correct_explanation: a near-maximum shared-memory allocation collapses active blocks per SM and prevents enough warps from running to hide latency
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: occupancy-limited
REASONING: The achieved occupancy is only 8.33%, which is extremely low and is the dominant signal here. This kernel allocates 24,576 floats of dynamic shared memory per block (~96 KB), which severely limits how many blocks can reside concurrently on an SM; meanwhile, DRAM bandwidth utilization is 0.0% and load efficiency is 100.0%, so it is not memory-bandwidth bound.
